mnemonics: Daisy Daisy Daisy - Daisy chain arbitration uses a daisy chain of devices connected in priority order.
Central Command - Centralized arbitration uses a single arbiter device to control the bus.
Independent Bidders - Distributed arbitration has each device independently decide who gets the bus based on unique IDs.
keyPoints: Bus arbitration resolves conflicts when multiple devices request the bus simultaneously.
The three main methods are daisy chain, centralized (using an arbiter), and distributed arbitration.
Priority determines which device gains bus access first when conflicts occur.
commonMistakes: Confusing daisy chain with distributed arbitration - daisy chain has fixed priority while distributed uses IDs.
Assuming all devices have equal priority - most systems implement priority schemes.
Forgetting that arbitration adds overhead - the process takes time and affects system performance.
